title: Don Smolenski

infobox.value: [Amherst College](/wiki/Amherst_College "Amherst College") and [University of Hartford](/wiki/University_of_Hartford "University of Hartford")

is an American businessman who is the team president of the [Philadelphia Eagles](/wiki/Philadelphia_Eagles "Philadelphia Eagles") of the [National Football League](/wiki/National_Football_League "National Football League") (NFL). He joined the franchise in 1998 as their [chief financial officer](/wiki/Chief_financial_officer "Chief financial officer") before being promoted to his current role in 2012.

**Don Smolenski** is an American businessman who is the team president of the [Philadelphia Eagles](/wiki/Philadelphia_Eagles "Philadelphia Eagles") of the [National Football League](/wiki/National_Football_League "National Football League") (NFL). He joined the franchise in 1998 as their [chief financial officer](/wiki/Chief_financial_officer "Chief financial officer") before being promoted to his current role in 2012.

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Don_Smolenski&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Smolenski grew up in [Pittsford, New York](/wiki/Pittsford,_New_York "Pittsford, New York").[[1]](#cite_note-1) He attended [Amherst College](/wiki/Amherst_College "Amherst College") and [Hartford University](/wiki/University_of_Hartford "University of Hartford").

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Don_Smolenski&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

In 1994, he was hired as the CFO of the [International Hockey League](/wiki/International_Hockey_League_(1945%E2%80%932001) "International Hockey League (1945–2001)").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

### Philadelphia Eagles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Don_Smolenski&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Philadelphia Eagles")]

In 1998, he joined the [Philadelphia Eagles](/wiki/Philadelphia_Eagles "Philadelphia Eagles") of the [National Football League](/wiki/National_Football_League "National Football League") (NFL).[[3]](#cite_note-3) Smolenski served with the Eagles as their CFO from 1998 to 2009, then as their chief operating officer from 2010 to 2011 before being promoted to team president.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7) He was part of the [team](/wiki/2017_Philadelphia_Eagles_season "2017 Philadelphia Eagles season") that won the [Super Bowl](/wiki/Super_Bowl_LII "Super Bowl LII") in [2017](/wiki/2017_NFL_season "2017 NFL season") and [2024](/wiki/2024_NFL_season "2024 NFL season").[[8]](#cite_note-u266-8)[[9]](#cite_note-a252-9)
